scenario: A team uses Desktop Modeler for offline / local BPMN authoring with Git as their version control. Meanwhile, the rest of the team uses Web Modeler for collaboration. A developer wants to share a BPMN authored in Desktop Modeler with the team via Web Modeler.
question: Which workflow brings a Desktop-authored BPMN into Web Modeler?

options.text: Git sync between Desktop Modeler and Web Modeler is automatic -- once a project exists in both tools, changes saved in one automatically appear in the other without any additional configuration. This is inaccurate. md:10), and supports specific Git providers (GitHub, GitLab, Azure DevOps, Bitbucket Cloud, Bitbucket Data Center). No automatic sync happens without this configuration.

bpmn file as an attachment to a teammate who then manually uploads it to Web Modeler -- while this technically achieves the goal, it is error-prone and hard to maintain: file versions can diverge, email attachments may be missed or overwritten, audit trails are lost, and reviewing who changed what requires manual tracking. The canonical paths (direct upload via Web Modeler's UI or commit to a Git-synced repository) maintain version history and make collaboration transparent.

bpmn file to a Web Modeler project (via Web Modeler's UI upload action), OR if the team uses a process application with Git sync configured, commit the file to the connected Git repository and let the sync bring it in. md:10 12 and supports 5 providers: GitHub, GitLab, Azure DevOps, Bitbucket Cloud, Bitbucket Data Center (per :18 25).

Desktop Modeler files are incompatible with Web Modeler -- Desktop Modeler uses a proprietary BPMN format that Web Modeler cannot import or open. This claim is inaccurate. bpmn files) as their interchange format. bpmn file can be authored in Desktop Modeler, committed to Git, imported into Web Modeler, edited further, exported back, and re-opened in Desktop Modeler without loss of fidelity. The open standard format is the bridge between the two tools.

a.text: Incorrect. BPMN is a standardised XML format shared between Modelers. To bring a Desktop-authored BPMN into Web Modeler: either drag-drop / upload via the Web Modeler UI, or use Git Sync (configure the Web Modeler project to sync with a Git repo, commit the file in Desktop / via git CLI, Web Modeler picks it up). The two paths suit different workflows: Upload is one-off; Git Sync is continuous integration.

b.text: Incorrect. Git Sync requires explicit project-level configuration.

c.text: Correct. Loses version history, easy to miss attachments. Use upload or Git.

explanation: Loses version history, easy to miss attachments. Use upload or Git.
